title: Share of artificial intelligence jobs among all job postings
subtitle: A job posting is considered an AI job if it requests one or more AI skills, e.g., "natural language processing", "neural networks", "machine learning", or "robotics".
caption: Across nine countries between 2014 and 2024, the share of job postings requesting artificial intelligence skills shows divergent growth trajectories. The United States line begins at 0.6% in 2014 and rises to 1.8% by 2024, maintaining the highest position throughout the period. The United Kingdom starts at 0.4% in 2014 and reaches 1.3% in 2024. Sweden, the Netherlands, and Spain all enter the chart around 2019 between 1.0% and 1.1%, then climb to approximately 1.3% by 2024. Austria begins near 0.6% in 2019 and ends around 1.1% in 2024. Italy starts at 0.5% in 2019 and finishes near 0.9% in 2024. New Zealand remains the lowest line, starting at 0.15% in 2014 and reaching 0.55% in 2024. All visible lines show an upward direction over their respective time spans, with the United States line consistently positioned above all others.
